summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer git identity to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint on install
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned identity files the package never wrote — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — to extract the developer's git email and GitHub login. It also collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform(), cwd, and CI provider details, then HTTPS POSTs the combined payload to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, a Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the package's declared homepage. The code self-describes as 'anonymous telemetry' while transmitting clear PII (OS username, hostname, git email, GitHub login). The package name and author branding ride on the official @openai/agents SDK to attract installs from developers expecting an OpenAI helper package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
